titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in China, ( Stuart ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in China, ( Stuart ) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Nanking , February 18, 1949—4 p. m.
[Received February 18—8:16 a. m.]

div.p:
417. Acting President told me he is summoning Sun Li-jenDirector of training of Chinese ground forces on Formosa (Taiwan). here with view appointing him Governor of Taiwan and making that island
basis for Chinese-American constructive cooperation. Ch’en Ch’eng wants to resignAs Governor of Taiwan. but Gimo preventing him. This one of many ways Gimo hindering Li’s efforts.

Li intends appoint Shao Li-tzeMember of the Kuomintang. premier if Sun Fo finally refused come Nanking. Should Shao be unwilling, will ask Ho Ying-chin be concurrently premier and Minister of National Defense. Reasons for choice of Ho is latter’s ability persuade WhampoaWhampoa Military Academy. clique support Li and perhaps influence Gimo.

Shao Li-tze telephoned Li peace talksBy a Shanghai civic delegation headed by former Premier W. W. Yen. began on morning February 16 and will continue several days with no public announcements. Shao feels rather optimistic, Li feels there one-third chance satisfactory results.
Sent Department 417, repeated AmEmb Canton 81, pouched Taipei.
